issue.title: fo76db.com
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Ads) [https://fo76db.com/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Ffo76db.com%2F) ### Comment > Ads not blocked on fo76db.com (Amazon PR carousels) > > ​Issue Description > ​Ads (Amazon PR/Affiliate Carousels) are still visible on fo76db.com despite having AdGuard (with HTTPS filtering) and various filter lists enabled. These elements seem to be using advanced anti-adblock techniques or dynamic DOM injection that bypasses standard CSS selectors. > ​Website URL > ​https://fo76db.com/ > ​Environment > ​Device: Sony Xperia 1 IV (Android) > ​AdGuard Version: AdGuard for Android v4.12.3 > ​Filter Lists: AdGuard Japanese filter, EasyList, etc. > ​HTTPS Filtering: Enabled (Certificate installed) > ​Symptoms > ​Amazon product carousels/lists labelled as "PR" remain visible at the bottom of the page or within content. > ​These elements appear to be injected via script after the page load. > ​What I've Tried (and failed) > ​Adding manual CSS rules like fo76db.com##.amazon-carousel or fo76db.com##.pr-container. > ​Using +js(set-constant, ...) scriptlets to block ad-injection. > ​Blocking QUIC (HTTP/3) protocol. > ​Element remains even with the latest filter updates. > ​Additional Context > ​I am a Lifetime Family license user. I've attempted to use the element picker to hide these, but they seem to regenerate with different classes/IDs. Please consider adding a more robust scriptlet or advanced filtering rule to the official Japanese filter list. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/6gukw0pwuc0804scg00ssookgo0occgqnww8cjfsc0.jpg?nc=1)

issue.title: fo76db.com
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Ads) [https://fo76db.com/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Ffo76db.com%2F) ### Comment > Ads not blocked on fo76db.com (Amazon PR carousels) > > ​Issue Description > ​Ads (Amazon PR/Affiliate Carousels) are still visible on fo76db.com despite having AdGuard (with HTTPS filtering) and various filter lists enabled. These elements seem to be using advanced anti-adblock techniques or dynamic DOM injection that bypasses standard CSS selectors. > ​Website URL > ​https://fo76db.com/ > ​Environment > ​Device: Sony Xperia 1 IV (Android) > ​AdGuard Version: AdGuard for Android v4.12.3 > ​Filter Lists: AdGuard Japanese filter, EasyList, etc. > ​HTTPS Filtering: Enabled (Certificate installed) > ​Symptoms > ​Amazon product carousels/lists labelled as "PR" remain visible at the bottom of the page or within content. > ​These elements appear to be injected via script after the page load. > ​What I've Tried (and failed) > ​Adding manual CSS rules like fo76db.com##.amazon-carousel or fo76db.com##.pr-container. > ​Using +js(set-constant, ...) scriptlets to block ad-injection. > ​Blocking QUIC (HTTP/3) protocol. > ​Element remains even with the latest filter updates. > ​Additional Context > ​I am a Lifetime Family license user. I've attempted to use the element picker to hide these, but they seem to regenerate with different classes/IDs. Please consider adding a more robust scriptlet or advanced filtering rule to the official Japanese filter list. ### Screenshots

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Anti Adblock Script) [https://10.com.au/race-across-the-world](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2F10.com.au%2Frace-across-the-world) ### Comment > When I enable the AdBlock on 10Play, I'm met with this ''Unexpected Error" which prevents the videos from playing. Also, when I turn off AdBlock I have no issue playing videos which leads me to believe that it isn't something external causing the issue. This problem is the same for all videos on the site. comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

The only way to show the menu is to click the menu item, which causes the page to refresh and closes the menu again. Disabling adguard will fix the problem briefly, but then it comes back. issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

<details>
<summary>Detailed description for iOS users</summary>

1. Install [**Usercripts**](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/userscripts/id1463298887) app
2. Enable **Usercripts** extension in Safari settings
	* iOS 18+: `System settings` => `Apps` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	* iOS 17 and older: `System settings` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	Find **Usercripts**, enable it and allow `On other sites` permission
4. Open the [tinyShield ](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@filteringdev/tinyshield@latest/dist/tinyShield.user.js) userscript URL in browser
5. Press the extensions icon in the address bar of Safari and select Userscripts 
6. Tap to install
7. In opened popup, scroll down and press Install button
8. Done.

</details>

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/issues).

issue.title: fo76db.com

labels.name: A: In progress

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Ads) [https://fo76db.com/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Ffo76db.com%2F) ### Comment > Ads not blocked on fo76db.com (Amazon PR carousels) > > ​Issue Description > ​Ads (Amazon PR/Affiliate Carousels) are still visible on fo76db.com despite having AdGuard (with HTTPS filtering) and various filter lists enabled. These elements seem to be using advanced anti-adblock techniques or dynamic DOM injection that bypasses standard CSS selectors. > ​Website URL > ​https://fo76db.com/ > ​Environment > ​Device: Sony Xperia 1 IV (Android) > ​AdGuard Version: AdGuard for Android v4.12.3 > ​Filter Lists: AdGuard Japanese filter, EasyList, etc. > ​HTTPS Filtering: Enabled (Certificate installed) > ​Symptoms > ​Amazon product carousels/lists labelled as "PR" remain visible at the bottom of the page or within content. > ​These elements appear to be injected via script after the page load. > ​What I've Tried (and failed) > ​Adding manual CSS rules like fo76db.com##.amazon-carousel or fo76db.com##.pr-container. > ​Using +js(set-constant, ...) scriptlets to block ad-injection. > ​Blocking QUIC (HTTP/3) protocol. > ​Element remains even with the latest filter updates. > ​Additional Context > ​I am a Lifetime Family license user. I've attempted to use the element picker to hide these, but they seem to regenerate with different classes/IDs. Please consider adding a more robust scriptlet or advanced filtering rule to the official Japanese filter list. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/6gukw0pwuc0804scg00ssookgo0occgqnww8cjfsc0.jpg?nc=1)

issue.title: www.welt.de

labels.name: T: Incorrect Blocking
labels.description: Website or app not working properly due to filtering

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

<details>
<summary>Detailed description for iOS users</summary>

1. Install [**Usercripts**](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/userscripts/id1463298887) app
2. Enable **Usercripts** extension in Safari settings
	* iOS 18+: `System settings` => `Apps` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	* iOS 17 and older: `System settings` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	Find **Usercripts**, enable it and allow `On other sites` permission
4. Open the [tinyShield ](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@filteringdev/tinyshield@latest/dist/tinyShield.user.js) userscript URL in browser
5. Press the extensions icon in the address bar of Safari and select Userscripts 
6. Tap to install
7. In opened popup, scroll down and press Install button
8. Done.

</details>

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/issues).

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 4 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.title: fo76db.com

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Ads) [https://fo76db.com/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Ffo76db.com%2F) ### Comment > Ads not blocked on fo76db.com (Amazon PR carousels) > > ​Issue Description > ​Ads (Amazon PR/Affiliate Carousels) are still visible on fo76db.com despite having AdGuard (with HTTPS filtering) and various filter lists enabled. These elements seem to be using advanced anti-adblock techniques or dynamic DOM injection that bypasses standard CSS selectors. > ​Website URL > ​https://fo76db.com/ > ​Environment > ​Device: Sony Xperia 1 IV (Android) > ​AdGuard Version: AdGuard for Android v4.12.3 > ​Filter Lists: AdGuard Japanese filter, EasyList, etc. > ​HTTPS Filtering: Enabled (Certificate installed) > ​Symptoms > ​Amazon product carousels/lists labelled as "PR" remain visible at the bottom of the page or within content. > ​These elements appear to be injected via script after the page load. > ​What I've Tried (and failed) > ​Adding manual CSS rules like fo76db.com##.amazon-carousel or fo76db.com##.pr-container. > ​Using +js(set-constant, ...) scriptlets to block ad-injection. > ​Blocking QUIC (HTTP/3) protocol. > ​Element remains even with the latest filter updates. > ​Additional Context > ​I am a Lifetime Family license user. I've attempted to use the element picker to hide these, but they seem to regenerate with different classes/IDs. Please consider adding a more robust scriptlet or advanced filtering rule to the official Japanese filter list. ### Screenshots

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://vk.com/audio-2001689051_148689051](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fvk.com%2Faudio-2001689051_148689051) ### Comment > Выключается плеер вконтакте когда включен Adguard. Когда выключен - появляется. Музыка играет по меню сверху или при нажатии кнопки "слушать". Вероятно из за того что гении из команды ВК что то изменили в коде виджета плеера и там по умолчанию должна крутиться реклама. > > The VKontakte player turns off when Adguard is turned on. When it's turned off, it appears. Music plays from the menu above or when you press the "listen" button. Probably because the geniuses from the VK team changed something in the code of the player widget and advertising should run there by default. ### Screenshots

issue.title: www.youtube.com

labels.name: T: Incorrect Blocking
labels.description: Website or app not working properly due to filtering

labels.name: T: Social Widget
labels.description: Social widgets, share, like buttons and popups

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# ATTENTION

> [!NOTE]  
> YouTube changes their detection scripts regularly. That means that sometime you can face the detection message.
> If all the following instructions do not work, please **email filters@adguard.com** or wait until any update is available. 

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use another adblocker in the same time because using another adblocker can delay/lags the YouTube loading (e.g., AdBlock Plus and AdBlock)[^1] OR interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use an userscript related to the bypassing YouTube detection message like [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing).
> Recently, YouTube anti-adblock script added infinite dummy advertisements to try to neutralize the userscript. ( [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing#264](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing/issues/264) )

> [!WARNING]
> Please check that there no another web browser extension that has adblocking feature is enabled on your web browser. It can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> Saving memory feature of your web browser or sleep feature of your operating system can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.
> In this case, please re-enable AdGuard's filtering by toggling the protection toggle.
> Following the instructions can be needed.

### The instructions
1. Force to update the filters by clicking the reload button in AdGuard Browser Extension/AdGuard mobile or update tray menu in your system tray. [^2]
2. Reload the YouTube webpage without cache by pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd> [^3].
3. Clear cache, cookies and site data of your web browser.
4. Re-open your web browser completely (checking your system monitor[^4] will be better).

[^1]: https://gitlab.com/adblockinc/ext/adblockplus/adblockplusui/-/issues/1576
[^2]: [AdGuard for Windows](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-windows/overview/#tray-menu); [AdGuard for Mac](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-mac/overview/#updates-check); 
[^3]: On macOS, <kbd>Command</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd>
[^4]: Task Manager on Windows, System Monitor on GNOME Shell, KDE System Guard on KDE Plasma or Activity Monitor on macOS. 


Previous issue https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/198973

comment.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [NSFW] [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EX_8ZjT2sO4&list=RDEX_8ZjT2sO4&start_radio=1](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.youtube.com%2Fwatch%3Fv%3DEX_8ZjT2sO4%26list%3DRDEX_8ZjT2sO4%26start_radio%3D1) ### Comment > Title: Age-restricted YouTube videos cannot be played when AdGuard is enabled (confirmation UI missing) > > Description: > When the AdGuard browser extension is enabled, age-restricted videos on YouTube cannot be played because the confirmation prompt is incomplete. Specifically, the “I understand and wish to proceed” button does not appear. > > Steps to reproduce: > > 1. Enable AdGuard browser extension > 2. Open any age-restricted video on YouTube while signed into an age-verified account > 3. Observe the age restriction prompt > > Expected behavior: > The prompt should display the option “I understand and wish to proceed,” allowing the user to continue watching the video. > > Actual behavior: > The confirmation button is missing, preventing playback entirely. > > Workaround: > Disabling AdGuard and refreshing the page restores the missing button and allows the video to be played normally. > > Notes: > > * This issue is not related to YouTube’s anti-adblock detection messages > * It appears that AdGuard may be blocking or modifying a script or UI element required for the age verification prompt > > Additional info: > Screenshots showing the missing button (AdGuard enabled) and restored button (AdGuard disabled) are attached. > ### [NSFW] Screenshots [Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/efig5kqwm884ogcs08w40kk040cgk8c6bm91lwwqlw.png?nc=1) [Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/nqlbcsfveaogg84cow0c0k8400k4owgbzics632mw0.png?nc=1) ### System configuration | Information | Value | |-------------|-------| AdGuard product: | AdGuard Browser Extension v5.3.1.40 MV3 System version: | `Windows 11` Browser: | Chrome Filters: | **Ad Blocking:**

Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
### Issue URL (Ads) [NSFW] [https://youtube.com](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fyoutube.com) ### Comment > Ads appear frequently at the top of the Suggested Video column, and in the video frame before, throughout and after a video plays, often spaced apart by only a couple of minutes. This happens with numerous videos, and is no better with AdGuard turned on than it would be if AdGuard were not installed on this computer. > > I have other computers and tablets with the latest 2026 OS versions installed, which suffer the same problems when using Safari or Firefox. > > I do have another browser which blocks all Youtube ads, without needing AdGuard, but cannot use it on this computer.. ### [NSFW] Screenshots [Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/4uej2xt0v9gkck0oko4g8gkcwo4ko0onfrl3hp6zi8.jpg?nc=1) ### System configuration | Information | Value | |-------------|-------| AdGuard product: | AdGuard for Safari v1.11.24 System version: | `MacOS Mojave 1-.14.6 [The latest OS I can use]` Filters: | **Ad Blocking:**

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://meet.google.com](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmeet.google.com) ### Comment > Hello > Actually I am facing problem with Google Meet > When The Adguard is installed and turned on as extension I am unable to join Meeting or Create a new Meeting > When I am removing Adguard Extension I am able to Join meetings or I am able to create new one > > Steps to recreate > 1. Install Adguard Adblocker as Extension in Firefox Browser > 2. Visit https://meet.google.com > 3. Try to create a New Meeting Or join a meeting with meeting link ``` Username: @Neelopal15 ``` ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/5bwt6hsx26g4w0wkss44c4cgwgo0k046vwybg38xtw.png?nc=1)
Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/7a15ypsswxs0gs4skckosg48k08cw8wncbhjp5xho0.png?nc=1)
### System configuration | Information | Value | |-------------|-------| AdGuard product: | AdGuard Browser Extension v5.3.1.7 MV2 System version: | `Windows 11 25H2` Browser: | Firefox Filters: | **Ad Blocking:**
AdGuard Base filter

**Privacy:**
AdGuard Tracking Protection filter

**Social Widgets:**
AdGuard Social Media filter

**Annoyances:**
AdGuard Cookie Notices filter,
AdGuard Popups filter,
AdGuard Mobile App Banners filter,
AdGuard Other Annoyances filter,
AdGuard Widgets filter

**Security:**
Online Malicious URL Blocklist Filters last update: | 2026-04-18T16:13:42 Tracking protection: | **General**:
Block trackers,
Strip URLs from tracking parameters,
Hide your search queries,
Send Do-Not-Track header

**Tracking Methods**:
Self-destructing third-party cookies (2880h)

**Browser API**:
Block WebRTC Browsing Security: | enabled Browsing Security statistics: | enabled ### Issue configuration [Import](https://reports.adguard.com/settings/import.html?product_type=ext&product_version=5.3.1.7&system_version=Windows+11+25H2&manifest_version=2&browser=firefox&filters=2.3.4.18.19.20.21.22.208&browsing_security.enabled=true&browsing_security.statistics_enabled=true&stealth.enabled=true&stealth.webrtc=true&stealth.strip_url=true&stealth.hide_search_queries=true&stealth.DNT=true&stealth.third_party_cookies=2880&stealth.block_trackers=true&filters_last_update=2026-04-18T16%3A13%3A42)
